Hacker News Confidence

Less annoying hacker news. (What is this?)

This site scrapes Hacker News once every 30 minutes, then sorts according to this formula, with upvotes as positive signals and comments as negative ones. In my experience, the best articles to read on HN are the ones with a high upvote-to-discussion ratio. Mostly because controversial pieces tend to produce a disproportionate number of comments compared to upvotes.


  1. Megadev: A Development Kit for the Sega Mega Drive and Mega CD Hardware 44 upvotes, 0 comments
  2. Baochip-1x: What It Is, Why I'm Doing It Now and How It Came About 96 upvotes, 14 comments
  3. Can I run AI locally? 1282 upvotes, 314 comments
  4. Secure Secrets Management for Cursor Cloud Agents 19 upvotes, 1 comments
  5. Show HN: Channel Surfer – Watch YouTube like it’s cable TV 543 upvotes, 161 comments
  6. Mouser: An open source alternative to Logi-Plus mouse software 352 upvotes, 108 comments
  7. I found 39 Algolia admin keys exposed across open source documentation sites 139 upvotes, 37 comments
  8. How Lego builds a new Lego set 28 upvotes, 4 comments
  9. You gotta think outside the hypercube 84 upvotes, 23 comments
  10. 1M context is now generally available for Opus 4.6 and Sonnet 4.6 867 upvotes, 331 comments
  11. Hammerspoon 305 upvotes, 112 comments
  12. Michael Faraday: Scientist and Nonconformist (1996) 17 upvotes, 2 comments
  13. Atari 2600 BASIC Programming (2015) 36 upvotes, 9 comments
  14. The Isolation Trap: Erlang 83 upvotes, 28 comments
  15. Python: The Optimization Ladder 27 upvotes, 6 comments
  16. Optimizing Content for Agents 58 upvotes, 21 comments
  17. Games with loot boxes to get minimum 16 age rating across Europe 242 upvotes, 143 comments
  18. A Survival Guide to a PhD (2016) 131 upvotes, 76 comments
  19. XML Is a Cheap DSL 80 upvotes, 44 comments
  20. AEP (API Design Standard and Tooling Ecosystem) 22 upvotes, 8 comments
  21. The Browser Becomes Your WordPress 12 upvotes, 3 comments
  22. Recursive Problems Benefit from Recursive Solutions 32 upvotes, 15 comments
  23. Qatar helium shutdown puts chip supply chain on a two-week clock 641 upvotes, 547 comments
  24. RAM kits are now sold with one fake RAM stick alongside a real one 57 upvotes, 40 comments
  25. Digg is gone again 247 upvotes, 232 comments
  26. Please Do Not A/B Test My Workflow 122 upvotes, 126 comments
  27. Coding after coders: The end of computer programming as we know it? 152 upvotes, 221 comments
  28. I beg you to follow Crocker's Rules, even if you will be rude to me 95 upvotes, 140 comments
  29. Wired headphone sales are exploding 216 upvotes, 360 comments
  30. Mothers Defense (YC X26) Is Hiring in Austin 0 upvotes, 0 comments

Last generated 10:00pm, 14 March 2026. Source here.
